Left frontal lobe, Left parietal lobe, Left temporal lobe, Left occipital lobe, Left insular lobe

the left frontal lobe, a nodular lesion is noted, slightly hypointense in T1 and hyperintense in T2. The right temporal region contains a nodular lesion, isointense in both T1 and T2. The right parietal lobe shows a patchy lesion, hypointense in T1 and hyperintense in T2, with FLAIR demonstrating hypointense internally and hyperintense externally. Bilaterally in the frontal and parietal lobes, multiple patchy lesions are hypointense in T1 and hyperintense in T2. Multiple cap-like and linear lesions are present in both lateral ventricle anterior and posterior horns and periventricular areas, also hypointense in T1 and hyperintense in T2. Both cerebellar hemispheres exhibit multiple small patchy lesions, hypointense in T1 and hyperintense in T2. FLAIR reveals slightly hyperintense in the left frontal lobe, equal signal in the right temporal region, slightly higher signal in the right parietal lobe, high signal bilaterally in the frontal and parietal lobes, high signal in both lateral ventricle anterior and posterior horns and periventricular areas, and low signal in both cerebellar hemispheres. No significant enhancement after contrast is observed in the bilateral frontal and parietal lobes, with mild uniform enhancement in the right temporal region and mild to moderate progressive enhancement in the left frontal lobe. The gray-white matter boundaries are clear, the ventricular system is normal in size and shape
